_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize GA batch: 5 north/metro-Atlanta legislators (mcdonald HD26-R thin, dolezal SD27-R now 2026 R nominee for LT GOVERNOR, hawkins HD27-R dentist, cox HD28-R newer member, donzella-james SD28-D w/ SB163 gun-preemption deviation). State tier. Evidence-first; null where no cited evidence; every TRUE/FALSE cited; party-line marquee votes credited per runbook. Aggregator (FreedomIndex) directional claims VERIFIED against FastDemocracy roll calls where rubric-critical; FreedomIndex's SB233=NO entry for Hawkins is a known error (official roll = YES) and was discarded.

profile.religion: Christian (sources conflict: Cumming First United Methodist Church vs. First Redeemer Church, Cumming — note ambiguity)
profile.background: State representative HD-26 (Forsyth Co./Cumming, north Atlanta metro) since Jan 2021; reelected Nov 2024 (def. Lakiea Bailey). Owner, McDonald & Son Funeral Home (Cumming); former Forsyth Co. Coroner (~15 yrs) and 22+ yr volunteer firefighter/EMT. UGA BA Political Science; A.S. Mortuary Science. Son of longtime GA PSC member Lauren 'Bubba' McDonald Jr. Committees incl. Appropriations, Public Safety & Homeland Security, Transportation (Vice Chair), Intragovernmental Coordination (Chair). Confirmed running 2026 — no candidacy change.

lauren-mcdonald@GA.notes_set: Low-profile rank-and-file R; scored from cited campaign self-statements (individual marquee roll calls not retrievable from accessible sources, so left null rather than inferred). SOFT campaign positions: 'Advocate for Pro-Life Policies', 'Defend the Second Amendment' + lists 'NRA Member' affiliation, 'Champion Public Safety' (27-yr first-responder career, Public Safety committee). Education messaging is PUBLIC-SCHOOL-FUNDING / LOCAL-CONTROL framing with NO stated voucher support — not scored as choice and not a deviation (no contrary vote documented) → null. GA Chamber B+ and self-described fiscal responsibility are non-rubric/vague → null. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Campaign Issues page lists 'Advocate for Pro-Life Policies' — affirmative pro-life self-statement (SOFT campaign self-statement).

Campaign 'Champion Public Safety'; 27-yr volunteer firefighter/EMT and former county coroner; serves on House Public Safety & Homeland Security — affirmative pro-public-safety posture (SOFT campaign self-statement).

1.note: Campaign 'Defend the Second Amendment' and lists 'NRA Member' as an affiliation — self-stated opposition to gun control (SOFT campaign self-statement; note: NRA membership is not an NRA candidate grade).

set.office: State Senator SD-27 (sitting · 2026 R nominee for Lieutenant Governor)

profile.religion: Evangelical Christian (founder of Passion City Church, Atlanta; former North Point Community Church staff; longtime Passion Conferences / Chris Tomlin tour manager)
profile.background: State senator SD-27 (Forsyth Co./Cumming) since Jan 2019. Senate Majority Chief Deputy Whip; Chairman, Transportation; Vice Chairman, Appropriations; Georgia Freedom Caucus member; on the Senate Special Committee on Eliminating Georgia's Income Tax. Co-founder/partner of Renewed Vision (ProPresenter church software). CANDIDACY UPDATE: NOT seeking SD-27 reelection — WON the June 16, 2026 GOP RUNOFF for LIEUTENANT GOVERNOR (def. John F. Kennedy); faces Democrat Josh McLaurin in the Nov 3, 2026 general. Record below scored on his sitting SD-27 tenure.

greg-dolezal@GA.notes_set: Strong-record conservative; refreshed candidacy (now 2026 R LT GOVERNOR nominee, not SD-27 reelection — runbook currency fix). HARD: PRIME SPONSOR of SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers, passed Senate 33-21, signed 2024); co-sponsor SB 390 (defund ALA-affiliated libraries); authored anti-DEI-mandate school law; co-sponsor SB 303 (revert to paper ballots / replace Dominion) — self-describes co-sponsoring more paper-ballot legislation than any member; co-sponsor SB 319 (constitutional carry); NRA A+ + PVF endorsement; YES on SB 499 (repeal silencer prohibition), SB 163 (firearm preemption), HB 1018 (2A protection), SB 572 (self-defense expansion). Own statements: 'Life begins at conception' + supported heartbeat bill (Faith & Freedom GA 100%); supports mass deportation / strong immigration enforcement. SB 140 vote and HB 1053/SB 202 individual votes not verified → null. SB 486 (foreign-law/Sharia ban) and income-tax-elimination advocacy don't cleanly map to rubric cells → null (not inferred). No cross-party deviation.

0.note: Campaign: 'Life begins at conception and must be protected'; states he 'proudly supported Georgia's heartbeat bill'; Faith & Freedom Coalition GA 100% (HARD self-statement + issue-group rating).

PRIME SPONSOR of SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship Act / $6,500 school-choice vouchers), passed Senate 33-21, signed 2024 — calls it the broadest school-choice bill in GA history (HARD sponsorship).

2.note: Authored a law 'stopping DEI mandates in public schools' (own claim) and co-sponsored SB 390 (defund ALA-affiliated public libraries) — opposes ideological curricula/institutions in K-12 (HARD authorship/co-sponsorship).

0.note: Co-sponsor of SB 303 to revert GA to paper-ballot statutes and replace the Dominion BMD system; self-describes co-sponsoring more paper-ballot legislation than any member — supports hand-counted paper ballots, opposes electronic voting machines (HARD sponsorship).

1.note: Campaign: supports 'strong enforcement of immigration laws, mass deportations' — 'If you enter the country illegally, you should be sent home' (HARD self-statement).

0.note: Co-sponsor of SB 319 (2022 constitutional/permitless carry) (HARD sponsorship).

1.note: NRA A+ rating and NRA-PVF endorsement; YES on SB 163 (firearm preemption of local gun-control ordinances) — opposes gun control/registries (HARD rating + vote).

2.note: Voted YES on SB 499 (repeal of the state silencer/suppressor prohibition) — rolling back firearm regulation (HARD vote per scorecard vote list).

3.note: Voted YES on SB 572 (self-defense expansion) — supports stand-your-ground/castle-doctrine-style protections (HARD vote per scorecard vote list).

profile.religion: Not publicly documented (no church affiliation located)
profile.background: State representative HD-27 (Hall & Lumpkin cos./Gainesville, north GA) since 2013; previously GA State Senator SD-49 (2007-10). Dentist (DDS, Emory); past president, Georgia Dental Association. Chairman, House Health Committee; also Appropriations Health subcommittee, Banks & Banking, Insurance, Regulated Industries, Rules. Reelected Nov 2024; confirmed on the 2026 GOP primary ballot — no candidacy change.

lee-hawkins@GA.notes_set: Documented conservative voting record (verified roll calls + scorecard vote list). HARD/verified via FastDemocracy: SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors) YES (House 96-75, 3/16/2023); SB 233 (school-choice vouchers) YES in BOTH the 2023 and 2024 House votes; HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary Criminal Alien Track & Report Act) YES (House 97-74, 2/29/2024). Per FreedomIndex vote list: HB 1084 (trans sports + CRT) YES, HB 1178 (Parents' Bill of Rights) YES, SB 36 (GA RFRA) YES, HB 1053 (anti-CBDC) YES, SB 420 (foreign farmland) YES, HR 780 (citizen-only voting) YES, SB 1 + SB 345 (vaccine-mandate bans) YES, SB 319 (constitutional carry) YES. NOTE: FreedomIndex erroneously lists SB 233 as 'No' — official roll calls show YES; the error was discarded. FreedomIndex composite 44% is low for an R but reflects spending/health-bill scoring + that data error, not a social-issue deviation. HB 481 (abortion) and SB 202 (elections) individual votes not independently verifiable → null. No cross-party deviation found.

2.note: Voted YEA on SB 140 (ban on gender-transition procedures for minors), House passage 96-75, 3/16/2023 — verified in the YES column (HARD).

4.note: Voted YES on HB 1084 (transgender-sports ban + CRT restrictions) — opposes LGBTQ promotion in school sports/policy (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Voted YEA on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship / school-choice vouchers) in BOTH the 2023 and 2024 House votes — verified in the YES column (HARD; FreedomIndex's 'No' entry is an error).

1.note: Voted YES on HB 1178 (Parents' Bill of Rights — curriculum access / parental authority) (HARD per scorecard vote list).

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1084, whose CRT-restriction component limits 'divisive concepts' instruction in K-12 (HARD per scorecard vote list).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 36 (Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act, 2025) — conscience/religious-liberty protection (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Voted YES on HB 1053 (prohibition on central bank digital currency) (HARD per scorecard vote list).

1.note: Voted YES on HR 780 (citizen-only voting constitutional resolution) — citizenship verification (HARD per scorecard vote list).
Voted YES on SB 1 (2023) and SB 345 (2022) prohibiting COVID vaccine-proof/passport mandates — declined federal mandate pressure (HARD per scorecard vote list).

2.note: Voted YEA on HB 1105 (Criminal Alien Track and Report Act / anti-sanctuary), House passage 97-74, 2/29/2024 — verified in the YES column (HARD).

4.note: Voted YES on SB 420 (restrictions on foreign ownership of agricultural land) (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Voted YES on SB 319 (constitutional/permitless carry, 2022) (HARD per scorecard vote list).

profile.religion: Not publicly documented (no church affiliation located; co-chairman of the Georgia Israel Caucus)
profile.background: State representative HD-28 (parts of Forsyth & Hall cos.; resides Dawsonville) since Jan 2023; reelected Nov 2024 (def. Michael Henson). Small-business owner 31+ yrs; former HS/MS football coach and math teacher (Lambert HS). UGA BA 1994. Committees: Education (Vice Chair, Policy Subcommittee), Agriculture & Consumer Affairs, Transportation, Technology & Infrastructure Innovation (Sec.); Deputy Whip; co-chair Georgia Israel Caucus. Uncontested in the May 2026 GOP primary; faces Mateo Sanabria (D) in November — confirmed running.

brent-cox@GA.notes_set: Newer member (since 2023); scored from a mix of verified-vote-list entries and cited campaign self-statements. Per FreedomIndex vote list (HARD votes): SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors) YES; HB 1001 (income-tax cut 5.19->4.99%) YES; HR 780 (citizen-only voting) YES; SB 1 (vaccine-proof ban) YES. Sponsored HB 1379 (Foreign Funding Transparency & Accountability Act). SOFT campaign self-statements: 'Proud to be Pro-Life', 'Pro-Second amendment', supports more parental choice/involvement in education, supports law enforcement & crime prevention, 'standing with state leadership on securing the border', cuts spending/waste & does not raise taxes. CPAC 92% (2024); GA Republican Assembly 'C'/59 (moderate, no specific defection found). SB 233 (vouchers), HB 1105 (immigration) individual votes NOT confirmable → null. Two soft-yellow FreedomIndex flags (NO on SB 9 / HB 986 AI-election-content bills, framed pro-free-speech) are not clear deviations → not scored. No confirmed cross-party deviation.

0.note: Campaign: 'Proud to be Pro-Life' — affirmative pro-life self-statement (SOFT campaign self-statement).

2.note: Voted YES on SB 140 (ban on gender-transition procedures for minors), House 96-75 party-line, 3/16/2023 (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Campaign: supports 'parents to have more choice and involvement in their children's education'; opposes 'one-size-fits-all'; sits on Education Committee (SOFT campaign self-statement).

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1001 (income-tax cut 5.19%->4.99%); campaign: budget 'does not raise taxes' and 'cuts frivolous, unnecessary spending and government waste' — fiscal-restraint position (HARD vote + SOFT statement).

1.note: Voted YES on HR 780 (constitutional amendment limiting voting to citizens) — citizenship verification (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Campaign: supports law enforcement and 'advancing crime prevention measures' — affirmative pro-law-enforcement (SOFT campaign self-statement).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 1 (prohibits vaccine-proof/mandate requirements) — declined federal mandate pressure (HARD per scorecard vote list).

0.note: Campaign: 'Standing with our state leadership on helping secure Texas border' — supports border security/enforcement (SOFT campaign self-statement).

1.note: Campaign: 'Proud to be ... Pro-Second amendment' — self-stated opposition to gun control (SOFT campaign self-statement).

profile.religion: Unconfirmed (Wikipedia lists Catholic; not independently verified)
profile.background: State senator SD-28 (south Fulton/Atlanta metro). In the Georgia Senate 1995-2003, then continuously since Dec 2009 (special election to replace Kasim Reed); redistricted into SD-28. Retired federal employee; former Atlanta Public Schools teacher; radio co-host. Morris Brown College. Chair, Urban Affairs; Vice Chair, Interstate Cooperation; on Appropriations, Economic Development & Tourism, Education & Youth. GA WIN List (Democratic women's PAC) endorsed. Running 2026 Democratic primary (May 19) vs. Mel Keyton — confirmed active.

donzella-james@GA.notes_set: Long-serving progressive Democrat with a uniformly liberal documented record AND ONE verified cross-party deviation on guns. Contrary (HARD, verified roll calls / sponsorships): sponsored SR 136 (reproductive-freedom amendment) + SB 15 (remove 'unborn child' from natural-person definition), voted NO on HB 481 heartbeat ('Pro-Choice Champion'); NO on SB 140 (gender-transition ban, Senate Vote #192, 3/6/2023) and NO on SB 185 (inmate gender care, did NOT join the 4 Dem crossovers); NO on SB 233 vouchers (#155); NO on SB 36 RFRA (party-line); NO on HB 1053 anti-CBDC (#731, one of 11 NO); opposed SB 202 (party-line); NO on SB 63 cash bail (#75) and SB 92 prosecutor oversight (#117); NO on HB 1105 anti-sanctuary (#719); opposed SB 319 constitutional carry (party-line). DEVIATION (HARD, verified FastDemocracy roll #182): voted YES on SB 163 (firearm preemption of local gun-control ordinances, NRA-backed, 3/6/2025) — a pro-choice Democrat siding with the gun-rights position. METHOD NOTE: FreedomIndex's other 'conservative' credits for James are misleading — HB 428 (IVF) and HB 371 (public-school funding) align with her progressive record, not conservatism; SB 29 (DNA) YES is unverified at member level → all discarded except the verified SB 163 YES.

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: sponsored SB 15 (2023) to remove 'unborn child' from the 'natural person' definition and SR 136 (2023) 'reproductive freedom' constitutional amendment — rejects personhood from conception (HARD sponsorship).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on HB 481 (2019 LIFE Act / heartbeat); labeled a 'Pro-Choice Champion' (HARD).

2.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors), Senate Vote #192, 3/6/2023; also NO on SB 185 (inmate gender care, 3/3/2025) and did NOT join the 4 Democratic crossovers (HARD).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers), Senate Vote #155, 3/6/2023 (HARD).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: opposed SB 36 (Georgia RFRA, 2025), which passed the Senate 32-23 on party lines with all Democrats against (sitting Democrat on the recorded contrary side — party-line marquee vote).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on HB 1053 (prohibition on a central bank digital currency), Senate Vote #731, 3/21/2024 (one of 11 NO votes) — against the anti-CBDC position (HARD).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: opposed SB 202 (2021 Election Integrity Act, absentee photo-ID), which passed the Senate on party lines with all Democrats against (sitting Democrat on the recorded contrary side — party-line marquee vote).

DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on SB 63 (cash-bail expansion, Vote #75, 2/23/2023) and NO on SB 92 (Prosecuting Attorneys Oversight Commission, Vote #117, 3/2/2023) — against the tough-on-crime/anti-rogue-prosecutor position (HARD).

2.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary / Criminal Alien Track and Report Act), Senate Vote #719, 3/21/2024 (HARD).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: opposed SB 319 (permitless/constitutional carry, 2022), which passed the Senate on party lines with all Democrats against (party-line marquee vote).

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (HARD, verified roll call #182): voted YES on SB 163 (NRA-backed firearm preemption strengthening state override of local gun-control ordinances), Senate Vote #182, 3/6/2025 — a pro-choice Democrat on the gun-rights side (opposes local gun control).
